calculation.method: reported close and daily move from the AP closing-index ledger; daily moves are versus the prior regular session of 2026-06-23, and the at-close S&P 500 level is cross-confirmed by the Yahoo Finance close quote

text: US stocks closed mixed on Wednesday, June 24, 2026. The S&P 500 fell 7.24 points (0.1%) to 7,358.22 and the Nasdaq Composite fell 110.40 points (0.4%) to 25,476.64, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 182.06 points (0.4%) to 51,848.90 and the small-cap Russell 2000 rose 11.15 points (0.4%) to 2,986.63. Week to date the S&P 500 was down 1.9% and the Nasdaq down 3.9%; for the year the S&P 500 was up 7.5%, the Dow up 7.9%, the Nasdaq up 9.6%, and the Russell 2000 up 20.3%. The at-close S&P 500 level is corroborated by the Yahoo Finance close quote of 7,358.22 (-0.10%).

subject.topic: attempted rebound faded into a mixed-to-lower close

intraday_peak.note: Dow was within striking distance of its intraday record high before gains faded

value.streak: Investopedia framed the S&P 500 and Nasdaq as closing lower for a third consecutive session, confirmed by the close
value.basis: intraday peak levels are mutable snapshots from the Investing.com late-morning update and may differ from any later print

market_session: US regular session on 2026-06-24 (intraday peak ~11:40 ET, then close)
text: Stocks attempted to rebound from a bruising two-day tech selloff but gave up the gains. Around 11:40 ET the S&P 500 was up about 0.7% near 7,416.86 and the Dow up about 1.1% near 52,215.29, within striking distance of an intraday record, per Investing.com. Those gains faded: Investopedia framed the S&P 500 and Nasdaq as closing lower for a third consecutive session, and both did close down (S&P -0.1%, Nasdaq -0.4%) while the Dow held a 0.4% gain. The intraday peak levels are mutable snapshots and are carried at medium confidence.

subject.topic: positive breadth despite a lower S&P 500 close; Dow up, Nasdaq down

value.sp500: fell 0.1% even though more stocks rose than fell within the index
value.dow: rose 0.4% on its lighter technology weighting
value.nasdaq: fell 0.4% as technology stayed under pressure
value.leadership_drag: losses for several tech giants including Microsoft weighed on the market
value.small_caps: Russell 2000 rose 0.4% to 2,986.63 but remained below the 3,000 level it first closed above on 2026-06-22

text: Internals were mixed rather than uniformly weak. The AP reported the S&P 500 slipped 0.1% even though more stocks rose than fell within the index, with losses for several tech giants including Microsoft doing the damage; the tech-lighter Dow rose 0.4% while the Nasdaq fell 0.4%. The small-cap Russell 2000 rose 0.4% to 2,986.63 but stayed below the 3,000 milestone it had first cleared on June 22, a sign the recovery was only partial.

value.cerebras_note: first earnings report as a public company; guided current-quarter gross margins to narrow by up to 10 percentage points
value.basis: single-stock figures are intraday/late-session snapshots from the Investopedia live page (recorded ~3:35 PM EDT) and may differ from final closes

market_session: US regular session on 2026-06-24 (single-stock moves intraday/late-session)
text: Chip and memory names wavered after Tuesday's tumble. Per Investopedia's late-session update, Micron slipped about 2% ahead of its earnings, SanDisk fell about 3% and Western Digital about 5%, while Magnificent Seven mega-caps Nvidia, Microsoft and Tesla each fell more than 1%. AI chip designer Cerebras Systems tumbled about 19% after its first post-IPO earnings report guided current-quarter gross margins to narrow by up to 10 percentage points. These single-stock figures are intraday or late-session snapshots and are carried at medium confidence.

value.timing: results due after the closing bell on 2026-06-24
value.significance: closely watched read on AI-driven memory demand and the broader AI trade
value.ytd_context: Micron shares had rallied more than 250% year to date and fell about 13% in the prior session
value.intraday_move: stock extended losses ahead of the print

market_session: US regular session on 2026-06-24 ahead of after-the-bell earnings
text: The session's marquee catalyst sat after the close: Micron Technology was due to report fiscal Q3 (quarter ended May) earnings after the bell, a print Wall Street treated as a key gauge of AI-driven memory demand. Yahoo Finance noted Micron shares had rallied more than 250% this year and extended losses ahead of the report after falling about 13% the prior day. Investing.com put consensus at roughly $19.92 EPS on about $34.66 billion of revenue. The earnings outcome itself is not yet in this cycle's data; the pre-earnings move is intraday and carried at medium confidence.

value.brent_percent_intraday: about -4% to -5% across the Yahoo and Investopedia live updates
value.context: oil fell to its lowest level since the US-Iran war began, near pre-war prices, as tankers resumed transiting the Strait of Hormuz, though Iran and Oman were discussing tolls on the waterway
value.macro_effect: the decline eased inflation concerns and accompanied a fall in Treasury yields
value.basis: the AP Brent -3.8% is the closing-ledger figure; the dollar levels and intraday percentages are mutable live-page snapshots

text: Oil prices plunged to their lowest since the war with Iran began. The AP closing ledger put Brent crude down 3.8%; Yahoo Finance and Investopedia live updates showed Brent near $73 (about -4% to -5%) and West Texas Intermediate near $70 (about -4.4%), just above pre-war levels, as tankers resumed transiting the Strait of Hormuz even as Iran and Oman discussed imposing tolls. The AP noted the drop helped ease inflation concerns. Dollar oil levels and intraday percentages are mutable and carried at medium confidence; the AP Brent -3.8% close figure is high confidence.

subject.topic: falling Treasury yields, rate-hike worries, and the Thursday PCE preview

value.us2y_note: near its highest level this year
value.driver: selling pressure in tech resumed on worries about lofty valuations, heavy spending, and coming interest rate hikes, prompting profit-taking in AI-linked names

pce_preview.note: Thursday PCE expected to show headline inflation 4.1% (up from 3.8% in April) and core 3.4% over 12 months ended May, per a Dow Jones/WSJ economist survey

value.basis: yields are intraday live-page snapshots; PCE figures are forecasts, not releases

text: A backdrop of higher-for-longer rates kept pressure on tech. The AP said Treasury yields fell; Investopedia put the 10-year near 4.41% (down from about 4.50% the prior day) with the 2-year near 4.15%, close to its highest this year. Yahoo Finance tied the resumed tech selling to worries about lofty valuations, heavy AI spending, and coming interest-rate hikes. Investopedia flagged Thursday's PCE report, expected to show headline inflation of 4.1% and core of 3.4% over the 12 months ended May. Yield levels are intraday and PCE figures are forecasts, so this claim is carried at medium confidence.

subject.topic: S&P Dow Jones Indices announced changes to the Dow and the S&P 500

value.dow: Alphabet (GOOG) will replace Verizon (VZ) in the Dow Jones Industrial Average
value.sp500: Honeywell Aerospace, being spun off from Honeywell (HON), will replace Conagra Brands (CAG) in the S&P 500
value.announced: announced by S&P Dow Jones Indices late Tuesday, 2026-06-23

market_session: announced 2026-06-23, reported during the 2026-06-24 US session
text: S&P Dow Jones Indices announced marquee index changes effective Monday, June 29: Alphabet will replace Verizon in the Dow Jones Industrial Average, and Honeywell Aerospace, a spin-off from Honeywell, will replace Conagra Brands in the S&P 500. The change is single-sourced to Investopedia and carried at medium confidence.
